Title: e-Shram
OneLineDescription: National database and registration portal for unorganised sector workers to access social security and welfare.
DetailedDescription:
e-Shram is the National Database of Unorganised Workers (NDUW) run by the Ministry of Labour & Employment. It allows domestic workers, construction workers, street vendors, gig/platform workers, artisans, and other informal workers to register for an e-Shram card (with a unique ID) so they can be identified for delivery of social security benefits, direct benefit transfers (DBT), and targeted welfare programs. Registration can be done via the e-Shram portal, CSCs, or designated enrolment centres.

Expanded guidance:
How to apply: Register on the e-Shram portal (https://eshram.gov.in/) or visit a nearby Common Service Centre (CSC) with Aadhaar and basic details. The portal issues an e-Shram ID instantly after verification; helpline numbers 14434 / 18008896811 provide support in multiple languages.
State-level contact points / where to find: State Labour/Employment Departments and the e-Shram PMU contact page list state nodal officers and helpdesk details. See e-Shram 'Contact Us' / 'Who's Who' pages for official contacts.

Primary portals: https://eshram.gov.in/

Title: PM SVANidhi (PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi)
OneLineDescription: Collateral‑free micro loans and incentives for street vendors to restart and grow businesses.
DetailedDescription:
PM SVANidhi provides working‑capital collateral‑free loans to street vendors, initially offering a micro loan with subsequent higher tranches for timely repayment. The scheme includes interest‑subsidy incentives for timely repayment, rewards for digital transactions, and helps vendors gain formal recognition (vending certificates) and access to credit and training.

Expanded guidance:
How to apply: Street vendors can apply online at the PM SVANidhi portal (https://pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in/) or via the PM SVANidhi mobile app, or apply in-person at Urban Local Body (ULB) offices or authorized CSCs using Aadhaar/ID and bank account details. Loans are sanctioned by member lending institutions after LoR (Letter of Recommendation) and required documents.
State-level contact points / where to find: The PM SVANidhi portal and the municipal corporation/ULB of the vendor's city are the nodal contact points; PM SVANidhi guidance manuals list city nodal officers and implementation steps.

Primary portals: https://pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in/

Title: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
OneLineDescription: Micro‑credit loans (Shishu/Kishore/Tarun) for non‑farm micro‑enterprises and small businesses.
DetailedDescription:
Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (PMMY) provides loans to small and micro enterprises through banks, NBFCs and MFIs under three product categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishore (₹50,001–₹5,00,000) and Tarun (₹5,00,001–₹20,00,000). MUDRA acts as a refinance and promotion agency to broaden access to institutional credit for entrepreneurs, artisans, vendors and small service providers.

Expanded guidance:
How to apply: Approach a bank branch, MFI or NBFC that offers MUDRA loans or apply via the Udyam/Mudra-support portals (e.g., Udyamimitra/JanSamarth). Carry ID, address proof, business plan and bank statements; banks process Shishu/Kishore/Tarun loans per category.
State-level contact points / where to find: Local bank branches, Regional Rural Banks and State Cooperative Banks are primary contact points; Mudra's website lists application guidance and authorised lending institutions.

Primary portals: https://www.mudra.org.in/

Title: PMEGP (Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme)
OneLineDescription: Credit‑linked subsidy (margin money) for setting up micro enterprises in rural and urban areas.
DetailedDescription:
PMEGP (implemented by KVIC and other agencies) supports new micro‑enterprises by providing a one‑time margin money (subsidy) grant—typically about 15%–35% of project cost depending on location and beneficiary category—while banks provide the loan portion. Eligible applicants include individuals, SHGs, and registered institutions; the scheme also offers training and market support.

Expanded guidance:
How to apply: Apply online through the PMEGP e-portal (KVIC) or submit the application at KVIC state/divisional offices or partner banks. Applicants may need project reports and bank account details; KVIC provides online EDP training and project templates.
State-level contact points / where to find: KVIC's PMEGP portal lists state office helpdesks and emails for each state/division (see KVIC PMEGP helpdesk pages).

Primary portals: https://www.kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal/

Title: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maandhan (PM‑SYM)
OneLineDescription: Voluntary contributory pension for unorganised workers, delivering a small assured monthly pension.
DetailedDescription:
PM‑SYM is a voluntary, contributory pension scheme for unorganised workers (entry age 18–40). Beneficiaries make small monthly contributions (amount varies by entry age); the government co‑contributes in certain cases and the scheme guarantees a fixed pension of ₹3,000 per month after the beneficiary reaches age 60. Enrollment is through authorised banks, CSCs and the Maandhan portal.

Expanded guidance:
How to apply: Enrollment is done through authorised bank branches and Common Service Centres (CSCs) with Aadhaar and bank account details; small monthly contributions are auto-debited. Visit the Maandhan portal (https://maandhan.in/) or your participating bank for registration.
State-level contact points / where to find: The Maandhan portal and the Ministry of Labour & Employment contact pages list nodal officers and state-level coordination points for outreach.

Primary portals: https://maandhan.in/

Title: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
OneLineDescription: Short‑term skill training, certification and placement support for youth and informal workers.
DetailedDescription:
PMKVY (implemented by NSDC/Skill India) provides short‑term training, Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL), and certification for numerous trades relevant to informal occupations — tailoring courses to make vendors, artisans, domestic workers and small business staff job‑ready. The scheme covers training costs, assessment, certification and links trainees to employers or self‑employment opportunities.

Expanded guidance:
How to apply: Check PMKVY course listings on the Skill India / NSDC portals or visit a nearby training centre/State Skill Mission. NSDC provides course directories, helplines and grievance numbers; many training centres accept walk-in registration.
State-level contact points / where to find: Each State Skill Development Mission (SSDM) coordinates training on the ground — contact details and training centre lists are available on NSDC/Skill India portals and state government skill websites.

Primary portals: https://nsdcindia.org/pmkvy-overview

Title: DAY‑NULM (Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana — National Urban Livelihoods Mission)
OneLineDescription: Urban livelihoods program providing skills, SHG support, credit linkages and vendor rehabilitation.
DetailedDescription:
DAY‑NULM aims to reduce poverty and vulnerability of urban poor households by enabling access to self‑employment and skilled wage employment. Key components include formation of Self‑Help Groups (SHGs), skill training, placement services, access to micro‑credit, vendor support (vending zones, licensing), and shelters for the urban homeless. It helps informal workers, street vendors and urban artisans access skills and credit.

Expanded guidance:
How to apply: Urban poor can contact the local DAY‑NULM/ULB office to join Self‑Help Groups (SHGs), apply for skill training, or seek vendor rehabilitation and credit linkages. DAY‑NULM also works through city mission teams and designated nodal officers; many city portals provide downloadable forms.
State-level contact points / where to find: DAY‑NULM central portal lists state mission contact details; individual State/City NULM websites (example: Karnataka DAY‑NULM state mission page) publish mission directors and office addresses.

Primary portals: https://nulm.gov.in/

Title: Udyam Registration & MSME Support (including CGTMSE)
OneLineDescription: MSME registration (Udyam) plus credit‑guarantee schemes that help small enterprises access collateral‑free loans.
DetailedDescription:
Udyam is the Government of India's MSME registration portal that provides an enterprise identity and access to various concessions, subsidies and procurement preferences. Complementary schemes like CGTMSE (Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises) provide a credit guarantee that allows lenders to offer collateral‑free loans to micro and small units, making formal credit more accessible for artisans and small vendors.

Expanded guidance:
How to apply: Register your enterprise on the Udyam Registration portal (https://udyamregistration.gov.in/) using PAN/Aadhaar to obtain an MSME/Udyam certificate; this enables access to MSME benefits and schemes. For credit and guarantee support, approach banks or member lending institutions and mention Udyam registration.
State-level contact points / where to find: District Industries Centres (DICs), State MSME Directorates and MSME offices are the local helpdesks — the Ministry of MSME website and state MSME portals list these contacts.

Primary portals: https://udyamregistration.gov.in/

Title: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
OneLineDescription: Very low‑cost accidental insurance for bank‑account holders covering death and disability.
DetailedDescription:
PMSBY is an affordable personal accident insurance scheme (one‑year renewable) administered via banks and insurance companies. For a small annual premium, enrolled members receive accidental death and full disability cover (commonly up to around ₹2 lakh; amounts and conditions per official rules). It's a low‑cost risk‑mitigation option for informal earners and their families.

Expanded guidance:
How to apply: Enrol by visiting your bank branch, registering via netbanking/UPI (if supported), or by SMS activation where available. Enrollment typically needs a savings bank account and signing the auto‑debit consent/authorization form (premium ~ ₹20/year).
State-level contact points / where to find: The bank where your account is held is the nodal enrolment point; check the Ministry of Finance / JanSuraksha portal for insurer and bank lists and forms.

Primary portals: https://financialservices.gov.in/beta/en/pmsby

Title: Ayushman Bharat — Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY)
OneLineDescription: Large cashless health insurance cover for eligible poor and vulnerable families.
DetailedDescription:
PMJAY (part of Ayushman Bharat) offers cashless secondary and tertiary hospitalisation coverage for eligible low‑income families — providing cover up to ₹5,00,000 per family per year for listed medical procedures at empanelled hospitals. It protects informal workers and their households from catastrophic medical expenses and is accessed via beneficiary portals or empanelled health facilities.

Expanded guidance:
How to apply: Check beneficiary eligibility on the PMJAY beneficiary portal (https://beneficiary.nha.gov.in/) or contact the local empanelled health centre, Common Service Centre (CSC) or your nearest public health facility. Enrollment/verification is done via the PMJAY/State health authority and empanelled hospitals provide cashless treatment.
State-level contact points / where to find: National Health Authority (NHA) coordinates PMJAY centrally; each state has a State Health Agency (SHA) that implements PMJAY — state SHA contact details are listed on the NHA/PMJAY portal.

Primary portals: https://beneficiary.nha.gov.in/
